highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.119 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release makes `/config` settings like theme, editor mode, and verbose output persist to `~/.claude/settings.json`, so your preferences survive restarts. It also adds a `prUrlTemplate` setting so you can point the PR badge in the footer to a custom code-review URL instead of GitHub. This is a great quality-of-life upgrade for teams with internal review tooling.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.118 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds vim visual mode (`v`) and visual-line mode (`V`) to the Claude Code editor, making keyboard-driven editing much more powerful for vim users. The `/cost` and `/stats` commands are merged into a single `/usage` command with tabs, keeping the interface cleaner. You can also now create and switch between named custom themes directly from `/theme`.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.116 Released
highlights.summary: Resuming large Claude Code sessions (40MB+) is now up to 67% faster, which is a big win if you work on long-running projects and frequently use `/resume`. MCP startup is also faster when multiple stdio servers are configured, with resource template listing deferred until your first `@`-mention. These performance improvements make day-to-day use noticeably snappier.

highlights.title: An Update on Recent Claude Code Quality Reports (April 23 Postmortem)
highlights.summary: Anthropic published a postmortem addressing community reports of degraded Claude Code output quality, explaining what happened and what steps are being taken to restore reliability. This is essential reading if you've noticed inconsistencies in Claude Code's responses recently and want to understand the root cause. It also gives insight into how Anthropic monitors and responds to quality regressions.

highlights.title: Ask HN: How Are You Using AI Code Assistants on Large, Messy Legacy Codebases?
highlights.summary: This Hacker News thread gathers real-world developer experiences using AI coding tools like Claude on large, complex legacy projects — a common challenge that tutorials rarely address. Experienced developers share practical strategies for prompting, scoping tasks, and managing context limits when the codebase isn't clean or well-documented. A great resource if you're trying to get value from Claude Code beyond greenfield projects.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a fast-paced but comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code aimed at developers who want to get productive quickly without sitting through hours of content. In 30 minutes you'll cover the core workflow, key commands, and practical use cases so you can start applying it to real projects the same day. It's an ideal starting point if you've installed Claude Code but aren't sure where to begin.

highlights.title: Full Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners (Step-By-Step, 2026)
highlights.summary: A thorough step-by-step beginner tutorial for Claude Code updated for 2026, walking you through installation, setup, and real coding tasks from scratch. This is one of the most up-to-date video guides available, making it especially useful now that Claude Code has evolved significantly from earlier versions. Follow along with your own project to get hands-on experience as you watch.

tips_of_the_day: After updating to v2.1.119, run `/config` once to review and save your preferred settings — they'll now persist across restarts and apply project-wide, so you only need to set them once.

If you're tackling a legacy codebase with Claude Code, break your requests into small, file-scoped tasks rather than asking Claude to refactor everything at once — smaller context windows mean more accurate and reliable edits.

Use `/usage` (the new merged command from v2.1.118) at the end of each session to review your token and cost breakdown, so you can spot which types of prompts are consuming the most context and optimize accordingly.
